title: [$250] Prevent AppReview modal from showing when another modal is already visible

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
When the AppReview modal appears while another modal is already present, it causes layout issues. We need to add a check so that the AppReview modal is suppressed whenever any other modal is currently visible. **Current behavior:** AppReview modal can appear on top of or alongside other modals, resulting in broken/wrong layout. **Expected behavior:** If any other modal is already being displayed, the AppReview modal should not be shown. **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07HPDRELLD/p1777899221743389
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @shubham1206agra

title: [$250] Held Expense with Multiple Violations Is Automatically Unheld and Approved Without Prompt

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Needs Reproduction
labels.description: Reproducible steps needed

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @rafecolton **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Product](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C03U7DCU4/p1777650519842409) ## Action Performed: 1. Create a report with multiple card expenses 2. Ensure at least one expense has multiple violations 3. Put that expense on hold 4. Approve the report ## Expected Result: User should be prompted with options: Approve all expenses, OR Approve only unheld expenses and move held expenses to a new report ## Actual Result: Held expense is: Automatically unheld Approved without prompt ## MelvinBot response: I investigated the approval flow for held expenses. Here's what I found: TL;DR:​ The most likely cause is a client-side race condition where another violation action on the same expense overwrites the hold field in Onyx state before the approval check runs. When the client doesn't detect the hold, it sends full=true to the server, which auto-unholds everything — no prompt shown. How the flow works: 1.  When you hold an expense, putOnHold sets transaction.comment.hold via a safe Onyx deep-merge 2.  When you approve, the client checks [hasHeldExpenses()](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/9fe568d00b9c67cb60b989565a3571e04c1f0835/src/libs/TransactionUtils/index.ts#L1882-L1888) which reads transaction.comment.hold from Onyx 3.  If held expenses are detected → hold menu modal shown (approve non-held only vs approve all) 4.  If ​not​ detected → approveMoneyRequest is called with [full=true hardcoded](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/9fe568d00b9c67cb60b989565a3571e04c1f0835/src/components/MoneyReportHeaderPrimaryAction/useConfirmApproval.ts#L32-L58) 5.  Server receives full=true → [handleHeldTransactions auto-unholds everything](https://github.com/Expensify/Auth/blob/a19cbd2b909eeb1df3915bf7922d521134b3436c/auth/lib/Report.cpp#L9544-L9598). The server [defaults full to true](https://github.com/Expensify/Auth/blob/a19cbd2b909eeb1df3915bf7922d521134b3436c/auth/command/ApproveMoneyRequest.cpp#L290) when not explicitly set. Why "multiple violations on the same expense" matters: Several code paths that deal with violations spread the full transaction.comment object into optimistic Onyx updates (e.g., [dismissDuplicateTransactionViolation](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/9fe568d00b9c67cb60b989565a3571e04c1f0835/src/libs/actions/Transaction.ts#L611-L625)). This captures a ​snapshot​ of the comment at call time. If the hold was set concurrently or the snapshot was taken before the hold Onyx merge was processed, the spread overwrites comment.hold with a stale value (null/empty), making hasHeldExpenses() return false on the next check. Card vs manual:​ No difference — the hold/approval logic treats both identically. To confirm:​ We'd need the specific reportID to check server logs for whether ApproveMoneyRequest received full=true and whether "Held transactions found, unholding them" was logged. If rafecolton can share the reportID, I can check VictoriaLogs to verify the exact sequence of events. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @huult
